document.title: IEC 60601-1-2 EMC Test Report for ADD Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

sourceSummary.oneLine: Nemko Korea EMC test report NK-21-E-0391 for H&abyz ADD Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector, including HAD1717MCW detector module, under IEC 60601-1-2:2014.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports regulatory evidence for electromagnetic emissions and immunity compliance of the ADD digital flat panel X-ray detector in a professional healthcare environment.
keyPoints: Report number NK-21-E-0391 was issued on 2021-06-17 by Nemko Korea Co., Ltd.
The tested product is ADD, described as a Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector, with HAD1717MCW listed as the detector EUT module.
The product fulfils IEC 60601-1-2:2014 and EN 60601-1-2:2015 requirements.
Emissions testing used Group 1, Class A limits for CISPR 11 related mains terminal disturbance voltage and radiated disturbance.
Immunity tests including ESD, radiated RF fields, proximity RF wireless fields, EFT, surge, conducted RF, power-frequency magnetic field, and voltage dips/interruption were reported as pass.

concepts.name: X-ray detector intended use
concepts.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Intended Use
concepts.evidence: ADD (Digital Flat Panel X-Ray Detector) is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.

evidence.claim: HAD1717MCW is listed as the detector EUT module.

evidence.claim: The product fulfils IEC 60601-1-2:2014 and EN 60601-1-2:2015.
evidence.evidenceText: The product fulfils the requirements of - IEC 60601-1-2:2014 and EN 60601-1-2:2015.

evidence.evidenceText: Intended environment Professional healthcare.

evidence.claim: Rated input is 100-240 V AC, 50/60 Hz, 1.0 A; output is 16 V DC, 3 A.
evidence.evidenceText: INPUT : 100 - 240 V~, 50/60 Hz, 1.0 A; OUTPUT : d.c. 16 V, 3 A.

evidence.claim: Software versions listed are S/W XAS_W 1.0 and F/W 1.0.
evidence.evidenceText: Software version: 1) S/W: XAS_W 1.0 2) F/W: 1.0.

evidence.claim: CISPR 11 terminal disturbance voltages and radiation disturbance verdicts were pass.

evidence.claim: IEC 61000-3-2 AC-mains harmonics verdict was pass.
evidence.evidenceText: IEC 61000-3-2... AC-Mains Harmonics IEC 61000-3-2:2018 P.

evidence.claim: IEC 61000-3-3 AC-mains voltage fluctuations and flicker verdict was pass.
evidence.evidenceText: IEC 61000-3-3... AC-Mains Voltage fluctuations and flicker IEC 61000-3-3:2013/AMD1:2017 P.

evidence.claim: IEC 60601-1-2 immunity tests for ESD, radiated RF fields, EFT, surges, conducted RF, magnetic fields, voltage dips, voltage interruptions, and RF wireless proximity fields were reported as pass.
evidence.evidenceText: IEC 60601-1-2... ELECTROSTATIC DISCHARGE... P; Radiated RF EM fields... P; Electrical fast transients / bursts... P; Surges... P; Conducted disturbances induced by RF fields... P; RATED power frequency magnetic fields... P; Voltage dips... P; Voltage interruptions... P; IMMUNITY to proximity fields... P.

evidence.claim: Electrical transient conduction along supply lines was not applicable because the device is not intended for installation in passenger cars, light commercial vehicles, commercial vehicles, or ambulances with 12 V or 24 V electrical systems.
evidence.evidenceText: Not Applicable, device is not intended to be installed in passenger cars and light commercial vehicles including ambulances fitted with 12 V electrical systems or commercial vehicles including ambulances fitted with 24 V electrical systems.

evidence.claim: Basic safety and essential performance monitoring included image acquisition, no adjacent two-line or blank image, normal operation, consistent DC power, and normal manufacturer program operation.
evidence.evidenceText: Acquisition image is obtained as intended. The acquired image should not have two lines adjacent or be blank. Operation normally when operation is required. DC power is consistently supplied from POWER SUPPLY... program provided by manufacturer is operated normally.

evidence.claim: The risk management report referenced for IEC 60601-1-2 documentation is A-TCF-5-037.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk Management Report (A-TCF-5-037) P.

evidence.claim: An RF EMC test report with document number NK-21-E-0392 is referenced for radio equipment.
evidence.evidenceText: RF EMC Test report Doc no. NK-21-E-0392 P.

evidence.claim: The Class A CISPR 11 instructions-for-use note was checked against the Risk Management Report and passed.
evidence.evidenceText: ME EQUIPMENT and ME SYSTEMS that are classified class A according to CISPR 11... Risk Management Report (A-TCF-5-037) P.
evidence.pageTarget: CISPR 11 Class A Instructions for Use

reviewFlags.reason: Source metadata authoritySignal indicates MFDS/domestic, but the source text itself is a Nemko Korea IEC/EN EMC test report and does not identify MFDS review or approval.
The file path suggests a CE 4th document and domestic certification context, but the document text does not provide a CE certificate, MFDS certificate, or domestic authorization number.
Trade mark field is blank in the source text.
Serial number is listed as N/A.
Some attachment OCR/table text is noisy and contains inconsistent labels such as source frequency 60.000 Hz in a 230 V 50 Hz flicker section; extracted core claims rely on clearer report sections.
